stories.title: Starmer accuses Farage of 'desperate stunt' as he announces by-election
stories.summary: Outgoing PM Keir Starmer called Nigel Farage's resignation and by-election gambit a 'desperate stunt', saying the public should not be subjected to the cost of a political manoeuvre. Starmer had announced his own resignation as Labour leader on 22 June 2026, with Andy Burnham set to succeed him.

stories.title: Andy Burnham set to become UK Prime Minister after Starmer exit
stories.summary: Andy Burnham, former Mayor of Greater Manchester, is set to assume the role of Prime Minister following Keir Starmer's resignation as Labour leader on 22 June 2026. A Labour leadership election is underway with Burnham as the frontrunner.

stories.title: Burnham recognised for London's role in UK growth at parliamentary reception
stories.summary: Andy Burnham MP attended a London Parliamentary reception, acknowledging London's central role in UK prosperity and growth as he prepares to take office.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, PMQs, debates, legislation

stories.title: National Security (State Threats) Bill passes Lords amendment in Commons
stories.summary: MPs voted to agree a Lords amendment to the National Security (State Threats) Bill, moving the legislation towards its final form. The Bill deals with espionage, foreign interference and state threats.

stories.title: UK Aid Policy: Global Funding Trends debated in Commons
stories.summary: MPs debated UK aid policy and global funding trends in the House of Commons on 7 July, with contributions touching on the Labour government's international development priorities.

stories.title: Student Loans Committee Report: 'Broken and Unfair'
stories.summary: A House of Commons committee published a report concluding the student loans system is 'broken and unfair', agreeing with students and universities that fundamental reform is needed.

stories.title: Political rivals vow to boycott Clacton by-election after Farage quits as MP
stories.summary: Labour joined the Conservatives, Liberal Democrats and Restore Britain in refusing to field candidates in the Clacton by-election triggered by Nigel Farage. The move amounts to an effective cross-party boycott of what critics called a cynical political manoeuvre.

stories.title: Burnham urged to ditch 'dangerous' UK-US NHS drug deal
stories.summary: Andy Burnham is facing pressure ahead of taking office to abandon a controversial UK-US deal on NHS pharmaceutical pricing that critics have labelled dangerous to the health service.

stories.title: Immigration and Asylum Bill introduced to Parliament
stories.summary: The Government introduced a new Immigration and Asylum Bill giving the Home Office powers to recover costs from asylum seekers who have received support, as part of ongoing efforts to reform migration policy.

stories.title: Badenoch says Tories won't stand in 'fake' Clacton by-election
stories.summary: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch called the Clacton by-election 'fake', announcing the party would not field a candidate and criticising Farage for using the public purse to avoid parliamentary scrutiny of his finances.

stories.title: Conservatives join cross-party boycott of Farage by-election
stories.summary: Along with Labour and the Liberal Democrats, the Conservatives have ruled out standing in the Clacton by-election — an unprecedented cross-party move that leaves Farage likely to face only minor party challengers.

stories.title: Ed Davey: Farage is 'trying to distract' with by-election
stories.summary: Liberal Democrat leader Ed Davey accused Nigel Farage of using the Clacton by-election as a distraction from scrutiny of his finances, calling for the by-election to be delayed until after parliamentary investigations into Farage are concluded.

stories.title: Lib Dems refuse to stand in Clacton by-election
stories.summary: The Liberal Democrats confirmed they will not field a candidate in the Clacton by-election, saying it would be wrong to spend taxpayers' money on a process Farage is using to evade accountability.

topics.name: Reform UK
topics.description: Nigel Farage, party news and announcements

stories.title: Farage resigns as MP for Clacton and vows to fight by-election amid finances row
stories.summary: Nigel Farage announced his resignation as MP for Clacton following reports he failed to declare financial support from an ally and questions over an unregistered £5m interest in his finances. He framed the by-election as a 'people v establishment' contest, saying he would stand again.

stories.title: Farage fact check: the truth behind five of his resignation statements
stories.summary: The Guardian fact-checked five major claims Farage made in his resignation video, finding that several of his accusations against political opponents and the media do not stand up to scrutiny.

stories.title: Electoral Commission to investigate Farage over undeclared gifts
stories.summary: Labour asked the Electoral Commission to investigate claims that Farage broke electoral law by not disclosing gifts and financial support from allies, adding to the pressure surrounding his departure from Parliament.

stories.title: Scotland could freeze datacentre projects in challenge to UK's AI strategy
stories.summary: The Scottish Government is considering a sweeping moratorium on building new datacentres, which would put a key plank of the UK's AI strategy at risk and create tension between Holyrood and Westminster over tech investment.

stories.title: SNP calls Nigel Farage 'dangerous chancer' after resignation
stories.summary: The SNP condemned Farage's resignation and by-election move as the actions of a 'dangerous chancer', accusing him of treating Parliament and democratic institutions with contempt.

stories.title: SNP independence fund court case launched over donations
stories.summary: The SNP faces a legal challenge at the Court of Session as independence donors have launched group proceedings, arguing that donations to the independence fund were misused.

topics.name: Cabinet Office
topics.description: Government reshuffles, senior appointments, cross-department policy

stories.title: Government announces NHS 10 Year Capital Plan
stories.summary: The Government published its 10 Year Capital Plan to modernise NHS buildings, upgrade GP surgeries, and bring more care closer to patients' homes — a major cross-government health infrastructure commitment.

stories.title: Government publishes response on Future of the Post Office
stories.summary: The Government issued its formal response to the consultation on the Future of the Post Office, setting out plans for a strategy to be implemented from summer 2026 documenting progress and future actions.

topics.name: Elections & By-elections
topics.description: Upcoming or recent election results, polls

stories.title: Farage's rivals rule out standing in Clacton by-election
stories.summary: In an unprecedented cross-party move, Labour, the Conservatives, the Liberal Democrats, and Restore Britain all announced they will not stand in the Clacton by-election triggered by Farage's resignation, leaving him likely to face only minor party challengers.

stories.title: Ashcroft poll: How will Burnham compare to Starmer?
stories.summary: A new Lord Ashcroft poll examines public expectations of Andy Burnham as incoming Prime Minister, views on whether there should be a general election, and attitudes toward the new 'Restore Britain' party.

stories.title: Armed Forces Bill receives Lords second reading
stories.summary: Members of the House of Lords discussed the key principles of the Armed Forces Bill during its second reading on Monday 6 July. The Bill proposes to strengthen the Armed Forces Covenant by extending statutory duties to devolved and local government.

stories.title: Railway Bill has Lords second reading debate
stories.summary: The House of Lords held a second reading debate on the Railway Bill on 7 July, covering the general principles of the legislation as peers examined the Government's plans for rail policy.
